NEDA Lab
Nutritional Epidemiology · Data Analysis

Understanding diet, health, and populations through data.

We investigate how dietary behaviors, nutritional exposures, and population-level determinants shape chronic disease and health outcomes. Our work integrates nutritional epidemiology, clinical research, and advanced data analysis.

Nutritional EpidemiologyObesityCardiovascular DiseaseDietary Data Analysis

Welcome to the NEDA Lab

Our laboratory is based in the Department of Food and Nutrition at Sookmyung Women's University. We use large-scale population data and rigorous epidemiologic methods to answer practical questions in nutrition and public health.

Research Approach

Our methodological identity is grounded in Professor Jane J. Lee's corresponding-author research, which combines rigorous evidence synthesis with large-scale, population-based epidemiology.

Rigorous Evidence Synthesis

Systematic reviews and meta-analyses are conducted according to PRISMA guidelines and registered in PROSPERO, with structured risk-of-bias assessment and meta-regression to explain heterogeneity across studies.

ESC Heart Failure, 2026

Large-Scale, Population-Based Epidemiology

Environmental and cardiovascular exposures are studied using nationally representative cohort data, linking exposure measures to health outcomes such as cognitive function and sarcopenia in community-dwelling adults.

International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 2022

Research

Our research interests are derived from Professor Jane J. Lee’s published work in cardiovascular, nutritional, environmental, and evidence-based epidemiology.

Cardiovascular and Cardiometabolic Epidemiology

Investigating cardiovascular and metabolic disease risk factors, treatments, and clinical outcomes using cohort studies and clinical research data.

Nutrition and Cardiometabolic Health

Examining how dietary factors, foods, and nutritional exposures are associated with obesity, diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and mortality.

Evidence Synthesis and Research Analytics

Integrating scientific evidence through systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and bibliometric analyses to evaluate health outcomes and emerging research trends.

Sustainable Food and Functional Ingredients

Evaluating the nutritional value, bioactive properties, health effects, and sustainable applications of foods and functional ingredients.

Environmental Epidemiology and Healthy Aging

Investigating the relationships of environmental exposures with inflammation, oxidative stress, sarcopenia, cognitive function, and age-related health outcomes.

Jane J. Lee, Ji Hyun Kim, Dae Sub Song, Kyoungho Lee. International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health, 2022.

Projects

Current research projects undertaken by the laboratory.

Ongoing Project · 2026–2029

Development of a Next-Generation Personalized Nutrition and Health Management System Using AI and 3D-Based Precision Anthropometry

This project aims to develop and clinically validate an AI-enabled personalized nutrition and health management framework that integrates three-dimensional precision anthropometry with multidimensional health data.

Year 1 · 2026

Precision Anthropometric Data and Body Shape Phenotyping

Establish a standardized three-dimensional whole-body scanning protocol, construct a precision anthropometric database, derive data-driven body-shape phenotypes, and identify anthropometric indicators that complement conventional BMI-based classification.

Year 2 · 2027

Development of an AI-Based Personalized Intervention

Develop a multidimensional Deep Health Profile integrating three-dimensional anthropometry, dietary intake, lifestyle factors, clinical biomarkers, genomic information, and gut microbiome profiles. Using these data, construct an AI-based personalized nutrition and health intervention and initiate a 12-month randomized controlled trial.

Year 3 · 2028

Clinical Evaluation and System Validation

Complete longitudinal follow-up and evaluate intervention effects on body composition, body-shape metrics, metabolic outcomes, and health-related behaviors. Validate the predictive and feedback algorithms and finalize the personalized intervention framework.
